provenance.source_corpus: Ten year responses of Engelmann spruce and a high-elevation Ericaceous shrub community to manual cutting treatments in southern interior British Columbia

source_spans.text: We studied the effects of manual cutting on an established vegetation community
Spruce survival was significantly higher in the treatment than the control 3 years after brushing (p = 0.020).
The cutting treatment significantly increased ground-level diameter after 3 and 5 years
Manual cutting reduced shrub cover for at least 10 years
Manual cutting increased Shannon diversity in the first year post-treatment (p = 0.039)
brushed spruce were taller relative to the vegetation canopy than control spruce throughout the 10 years assessment period
Shrub height was reduced in the first year post-treatment
negative relationship between first year total shrub or summed Ericaceous shrub abundance... and spruce diameter
